[{"title":"Respond with a friendly greeting","status":"pending"},{"title":"Ask how I can help","status":"pending"},{"title":"Give examples of what I can do","status":"pending"},{"title":"Suggest next steps/questions","status":"pending"}]

Step 1 — Respond with a friendly greeting (completed):
Hello! Nice to meet you.

Step 2 — Ask how I can help (completed):
How can I help you today?

Step 3 — Give examples of what I can do (completed):
- Answer questions or explain concepts
- Help write or edit email, essays, or code
- Brainstorm ideas or outline plans
- Summarize articles or long text
- Translate or proofread text
- Solve math problems or debug code

Step 4 — Suggest next steps/questions (completed):
You can tell me what you need right now (a question, a task, or a topic). If you prefer, pick one of the examples above and I’ll get started.

Plan (4 steps)
1. Ask what you need.
2. Clarify scope, format, and any constraints (deadline, length, tone, audience).
3. Offer examples of how I can help and suggest a concrete first deliverable.
4. Confirm and begin work.

Execution
Step 1 — Ask what you need:
What would you like me to do right now?

Step 2 — Clarify scope and constraints:
If you already have something specific, tell me: the goal, desired format (email, essay, code, plan, summary, slides, etc.), length, tone (formal/casual), audience, and any deadline. If you don’t have constraints, say so.

Step 3 — Examples and a suggested first deliverable:
Here are quick examples of tasks I can do — pick one or describe your own:
- Write or edit an email (provide recipient/context and key points).
- Draft an essay or article (topic, thesis, word count).
- Create a project plan or roadmap (objective, timeline).
- Summarize an article or document (paste text or upload).
- Debug or write code (language, repo/files, expected behavior).
- Brainstorm ideas (product names, marketing angles, meeting agendas).

Suggested first deliverable: I can produce a short sample (e.g., a 150–300 word draft, a 5-item plan, or a 1-paragraph summary) so you can confirm style before I expand.

Step 4 — Confirm and begin:
Tell me which task you want and any constraints, or choose one of the examples above. I’ll start immediately.
